raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 566 would require web browsers to offer consumers the ability to exercise their privacy rights through global opt-out tools.",
    "Many of the top web browsers do not offer opt-out preference signals for consumers.",
    "AB 566 would make it easier for consumers to state their privacy preferences by allowing a user to exercise their opt-out rights in a single step.",
    "Currently, eight states, including California, require businesses to honor browser privacy signals as an opt-out of the sale of personal information.",
    "The California Consumer Privacy Act gives California consumers the right to stop the sale and sharing of their personal information, including via opt-out preference signals.",
    "If AB 566 is passed, California would become the first state to require browser vendors to directly support opt-out preference signals."
  ]
}
```
